reviewSummary: This PR hard-switches `pickRemediationWorkerClass` to always return `'codex'` regardless of `builderTag`, drops the per-tag dispatcher logic, replaces granular dispatcher tests with a single bundled "always codex" test, and adds a runbook bullet documenting the override. The change is small and intentional, but it leaves the routing function with an unused parameter, deletes a regression test that previously caught a real bug, and the runbook overstates how `builderTag` is preserved relative to what actually lands in PR commit history.
reviewBody:
## Summary
This PR hard-switches `pickRemediationWorkerClass` to always return `'codex'` regardless of `builderTag`, drops the per-tag dispatcher logic, replaces granular dispatcher tests with a single bundled "always codex" test, and adds a runbook bullet documenting the override. The change is small and intentional, but it leaves the routing function with an unused parameter, deletes a regression test that previously caught a real bug, and the runbook overstates how `builderTag` is preserved relative to what actually lands in PR commit history.

## Blocking Issues
- None.

## Non-blocking Issues
- **Runbook overstates git-attribution preservation.**
  - File: `docs/follow-up-runbook.md`
  - Lines: 15
  - Problem: The bullet says "the durable `builderTag` is still preserved on the job for git attribution and history". But commit trailers are stamped by `hooks/worker-provenance-commit-msg` from the `WORKER_CLASS` env injected at spawn time, not from `builderTag`. With this PR pinning `workerClass='codex'`, every remediation commit on a `[claude-code]` or `[clio-agent]` PR will carry `Worker-Class: codex` in its trailers. The job-ledger JSON keeps `builderTag`, but anyone auditing the PR's git history will see codex authorship.
  - Why it matters: The cross-model invariant called out in `SPEC.md §Solution` ("the agent that builds the code is never the agent that reviews it") is review-side, not remediation-side, so this isn't a SPEC violation — but the runbook reads as if commit-level provenance is unaffected, which is wrong and will mislead future readers debugging an incident.
  - Recommended fix: Reword to e.g. "the durable `builderTag` is still preserved on the job ledger for downstream queue/audit purposes; commit trailers on remediation commits will reflect `Worker-Class: codex` because that is the worker class actually executing."

- **`pickRemediationWorkerClass(job)` parameter is now dead.**
  - File: `src/follow-up-remediation.mjs`
  - Lines: 559 (post-diff)
  - Problem: Function ignores `job` entirely. ESLint `no-unused-vars` (or strict TS check, if added later) will flag it. More importantly, the signature continues to imply job-aware routing, which makes the call sites at line 1427 (`resolveReconcileWorkerClass`) and 2322 (consume) read as if dispatch is job-driven when it isn't.
  - Why it matters: A future reader fixing reconcile-time bot-token selection may assume the function will react to `builderTag` and not realize the override is silent.
  - Recommended fix: Either rename the parameter to `_job` to signal "intentionally unused (LAC-358 hard-switch)", or keep `job` and add a one-line `// (job intentionally unused — see LAC-358 above)` so the next maintainer doesn't waste time tracing nonexistent logic.

- **Revert-criteria placeholders are aspirational, not enforceable.**
  - File: `src/follow-up-remediation.mjs`
  - Lines: 543–548 (post-diff comment)
  - Problem: "Revisit this only after the claude-code path shows no silent hangs across N consecutive [claude-code] PRs over M weeks" — `N` and `M` are literal placeholders. No telemetry or counter exists in the diff to make this gate measurable.
  - Why it matters: A comment that looks like a contract but resolves to "whenever someone feels like it" rots into permanent state. LAC-358 will quietly become the forever-default.
  - Recommended fix: Either fill in concrete numbers tied to a real signal (e.g. "≥10 consecutive `[claude-code]` PRs without `manual-inspection` reconcile state over a 2-week window"), or drop the language and say plainly "revert when feedback memory is updated to remove the trust gap."

- **Regression test for `[clio-agent]` misrouting is deleted, not preserved.**
  - File: `test/follow-up-remediation.test.mjs`
  - Lines: 666–720 (deletions)
  - Problem: The old test `pickRemediationWorkerClass routes builderTag=clio-agent to codex remediator (not claude-code)` was guarding a specific historical bug — "reverse-mapping reviewerModel='codex' → claude-code, which misroutes every [clio-agent] follow-up job." That regression surface still exists when this is reverted; deleting the test means the next person to touch this function loses the documented bug-shape that motivated the legacy fallback.
  - Why it matters: The hard-switch doesn't make the historical bug irrelevant — it just makes the test currently redundant. On revert, the `clio-agent` ambiguity returns and the regression test will need to be reconstructed from memory.
  - Recommended fix: Mark the per-tag tests `.skip` with a comment naming LAC-358, or move them behind a `if (process.env.LAC_358_REVERTED)` gate, so the contract survives the override window.

- **Bundled test fails fast, masks multi-path regressions.**
  - File: `test/follow-up-remediation.test.mjs`
  - Lines: 670–686 (post-diff)
  - Problem: All 8 routing cases live inside one `for` loop in a single `test()`. The first failing `assert.equal` aborts the loop; subsequent labels never run.
  - Why it matters: If a future refactor breaks 4 distinct dispatch paths, CI surfaces only the first. Granular `test(...)` calls would have surfaced all 4 in one run.
  - Recommended fix: Either keep the loop but accumulate failures (`const failures = []; ... if (actual !== 'codex') failures.push(label)`; assert empty at end), or split back into one `test()` per case.

- **Public-comment provenance shifts for never-spawned `[claude-code]` jobs.**
  - File: `src/follow-up-remediation.mjs`
  - Lines: 1422–1428 (`resolveReconcileWorkerClass`)
  - Problem: When `worker.model` is unset (e.g. `state='never-spawned'` reconcile path at line 2306), this falls through to `pickRemediationWorkerClass(job)` which now returns `'codex'` even for `builderTag='claude-code'`. Pre-PR, that path returned `'claude-code'`, which selected the claude-code bot token via `WORKER_CLASS_TO_BOT_TOKEN_ENV`. Post-PR, the codex bot will post the terminal comment on a `[claude-code]` PR that never managed to spawn a worker.
  - Why it matters: Subtle behavior change in PR-comment author identity; not called out in the runbook bullet. If operators triage by bot, a `[claude-code]` PR with codex-bot comments will look anomalous.
  - Recommended fix: Either acknowledge this in the runbook bullet ("public PR comments on remediation outcomes will also be posted under the codex bot identity for the duration of the override"), or special-case `resolveReconcileWorkerClass` to keep using `builderTag` for bot selection so attribution remains tag-faithful while execution moves to codex.

## Suggested Fixes
- Tighten the runbook bullet so the `builderTag`-preservation claim covers job-ledger only, and explicitly notes commit trailers and PR-comment author identity will read codex during the override.
- Either underscore-prefix the unused `job` param or add a one-line in-body comment so the dead parameter doesn't read as job-aware logic.
- Replace `N` / `M` placeholder revert criteria with a concrete signal or drop the language.
- Preserve the deleted per-tag tests as `.skip`, with a note pointing at LAC-358, so the contract survives revert.
- Refactor the bundled "always codex" test to either accumulate failures or split per case so CI surfaces all regressions in a single run.

## Verdict
Comment only

commentDelivery.body:
<!-- adversarial-review-remediation-marker:laceyenterprises__adversarial-review-pr-35-2026-05-04T05-24-51-401Z:r1:completed -->
### Remediation Worker (codex) — round 1 of 1

**Outcome:** `completed` — re-review queued.

**Summary**

```text
Tightened the LAC-358 codex-override docs, made the unused remediation routing parameter explicitly intentional, and restored granular routing coverage with the historical clio-agent regression preserved as skipped revert-time coverage.
```

**Validation run**

- ```text
Ran `git fetch origin && git rebase origin/main` successfully.
```

- ```text
Ran `node --test --test-name-pattern "pickRemediationWorkerClass|reconcile routes \[clio-agent\]|reconcile falls through to pickRemediationWorkerClass|reconcile prefers worker.model" test/follow-up-remediation.test.mjs` (11 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failed).
```

- ```text
Ran `node --test test/follow-up-remediation.test.mjs`; the full file still has two pre-existing failures on trunk in `spawnCodexRemediationWorker omits WORKER_JOB_ID when no jobId is provided` and `consumeNextFollowUpJob moves a claimed job to failed/ when claude-code OAuth pre-flight throws`.
```

**Re-review status:** queued — `The LAC-358 remediation-routing findings called out in review are addressed and the PR is ready for a fresh adversarial pass.`
